info.name: Get cost centers by legal entity

params.name: legal_entity_id
params.description: Legal entity id

items.docs:
Fetch all cost centers related to a given legal entity id
 **Token scopes**: `legal-entity:read`

params.name: id
params.description: Unique identifier of the group to be cloned.

items.docs:
Clone an existing group within the organization. This creates a new group with the specified name, copying the structure and settings from the source group.
 **Token scopes**: `groups:write`

params.name: limit
params.description: The maximum number of groups to return (between 1 and 100)

params.name: sort_order
params.description: Sort the results in ascending (ASC) or descending (DESC) order based on the group name.

params.name: cursor
params.description: The encoded cursor for paginated results. This is an opaque string that allows fetching the next set of results.

params.name: include_archived_groups
params.description: Include archived groups (soft deleted records). Defaults to `true`.

params.name: external_metadata
params.description: Extra information about the group.

items.docs:
List all the groups in your organization.
 **Token scopes**: `groups:read`

Create a new group within your organization.
 **Token scopes**: `groups:read`, `groups:write`

Edit the details of an existing group.
 **Token scopes**: `groups:read`, `groups:write`

Archive an existing group.
 **Token scopes**: `groups:write`

info.name: Get business visa eligibility and requirements

params.name: destination_country
params.description: The destination country code of the trip in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format

params.name: nationality
params.description: The nationality country code of the person in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format

params.name: residence_country
params.description: The residence country code of the person in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format

params.name: trip_start_date
params.description: The trip start date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD)

params.name: trip_end_date
params.description: The trip end date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD)

params.name: trip_reason
params.description: The reason for the trip

params.name: second_nationality
params.description: The second nationality country code of the person in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format (optional)

items.docs:
This endpoint receives personal information and desired travel information, analyzes and returns the possible business visa options for the given data.
 **Token scopes**: `immigration:read`

params.name: id
params.description: Immigration case id

items.docs:
Get detailed information for a specific immigration case. Use this to check case status and track process progress. The id path parameter is the case UUID returned from the 'Create Client Case' endpoint. Response is enriched with applicant profile, visa type, case status, estimated completion date, process details, and compliance document details.
 **Token scopes**: `immigration:read`

params.name: case_id
params.description: Filter by case ID

items.docs:
Get detailed information for a specific immigration case. Use this to check case status and track process progress. The id path parameter is the case UUID returned from the 'Create Client Case' endpoint. Response is enriched with applicant profile, visa type, case status, estimated completion date, process details, and compliance document details.
 **Token scopes**: `worker:read`

params.name: id
params.description: Immigration case document id

items.docs:
Fetch immigration document details by document id
 **Token scopes**: `immigration:read`

params.name: country_code
params.description: Alpha 2 country code

items.docs:
Fetch immigration supported visa types by country
 **Token scopes**: `immigration:read`

params.name: search
params.description: Search by applicant name or code

params.name: case_type_ids
params.description: Filter by case type IDs

params.name: statuses
params.description: Filter by case status

params.name: countries
params.description: Filter by country. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes (https://www.iban.com/country-codes).

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor for fetching next set of results

params.name: limit
params.description: Max number of results to return

items.docs:
Retrieves a paginated list of immigration cases with filters for applicant name, case type, status, and country. Returns case details including type, status, process, timestamps, and optional closure information.
 **Token scopes**: `immigration:read`

info.name: Retrieve a required document for a case

params.name: worker_id
params.description: The hris profile oid (public id) of the worker.

params.name: case_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the case for which the required document is being requested.

items.docs:
Use this endpoint to retrieve the details of a specific document requirement for a worker's case. This includes the requirement's status and information about any previously rejected documents
 **Token scopes**: `worker:read`

info.name: Retrieve onboarding right-to-work case for a worker

params.name: worker_id
params.description: The hris profile oid (public id) of the worker.

params.name: contract_id
params.description: Filters the worker's onboarding case to a specific contract. If the worker has multiple contracts and this parameter is omitted, the response may return a case from any associated contract; the response includes contract.id to indicate which contract was used. Provide this parameter for deterministic results.

items.docs:
Returns the right to work case for employee onboarding including the documents if any. In case no right to work case is open, the response will be empty.
 **Token scopes**: `worker:read`

params.name: worker_id
params.description: The hris profile oid (public id) of the worker.

params.name: case_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the case for which the required document is being requested.

params.name: document_request_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the document request fo which this document is being uplodaded

items.docs:
Upload a required immigration document for a specific case. The document will be submitted for review.
 **Token scopes**: `worker:write`

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of results to return per page.

params.description: Sorting order of the results.

params.description: Cursor for pagination.

params.name: include_archived
params.description: Whether to include archived legal entities in the results.

params.description: Filter by global payroll flag.

items.docs:
Retrieve a list of legal entities in your account.
 **Token scopes**: `organizations:read`, `accounting:read`

Create a new legal entity under an organization.
 **Token scopes**: `legal-entity:write`, `legal-entity:read`

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the legal entity to update.

items.docs:
Update the details of an existing legal entity.
 **Token scopes**: `legal-entity:read`, `legal-entity:write`

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the legal entity to archive.

items.docs:
Archive a legal entity. This marks the entity as inactive but does not permanently remove it.
 **Token scopes**: `legal-entity:read`, `legal-entity:write`

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of items to return per page.

params.name: order
params.description: Sorting order of the results.

params.name: sort_by
params.description: Field used to sort the results.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Cursor used for paginating to the next page of results.

items.docs:
List industry subcategories with their parent category details, supporting cursor-based pagination and sorting by category or subcategory name. Each subcategory is identified by a UUID and includes NAICS codes. Used during entity setup to select an industry classification.
 **Token scopes**: `legal-entity:read`

params.name: after_cursor
params.description: Cursor for pagination. Use this to retrieve the next page of results.

items.docs: Retrieve a list of pre-defined job titles in Deel platform.
Retrieve a list of countries supported by Deel, along with details about visa and EoR support, sub-territories, and their classifications.

params.name: is_eor_contract
params.description: when `true`, exclude C-level seniorities for EOR contracts. Set to `false` to return all seniorities

items.docs: Retrieve a list of predefined seniority levels for roles in the Deel platform, including their names, hierarchical levels, and unique identifiers.
Retrieve the list of currencies supported by Deel, including their ISO codes and names
Retrieve a list of predefined time-off types that can be registered in the Deel platform.

Use this endpoint to generate secure, time-limited magic links that enable password-free, seamless worker authentication for quick and safe access. Ideal for temporary sessions or low-friction login flows.
 **Token scopes**: `worker:read`

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of records to return in the response.

params.name: offset
params.description: The offset or starting point for pagination.

items.docs:
Retrieve a list of all managers in the organization along with pagination details.
 **Token scopes**: `organizations:read`

Create a new manager for the organization by providing the required user details. This endpoint returns the newly created manager's information.
 **Token scopes**: `organizations:write`

Creates a new organization along with:

- An API token scoped to the new organization. This token can be used to perform API operations on behalf of the new organization.
- A default group for the organization.
- A hierarchy linking the authenticated organization (the Parent Organization) with the new one (the Child Organization).
- A user from the parent organization who will be assigned to the child organization.

This endpoint sets up the bare minimum required for the new organization to be o

Retrieve details of the current organization associated with the authenticated user. The organization is automatically detected based on the authentication token provided in the request.
 **Token scopes**: `organizations:read`

Use this endpoint to create a manual identity verification submission (in case automated one has failed multiple times or document country is not supported by automated solution)
 **Token scopes**: `worker:write`

Create Veriff session
 **Token scopes**: `screenings:write`, `worker:write`

params.name: entity_type
params.description: The type of entity to screen. Only 'profile', 'hris_profile', 'legal_entity' are accepted.

params.name: entity_id
params.description: UUID v4 of the entity

items.docs:
Get the latest screening for an entity.
 **Token scopes**: `screenings:read`

params.name: country
params.description: The document's issuing country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)

params.name: document_type
params.description: The document to be provided during KYC

items.docs:
Get verification method by provided country and document type
 **Token scopes**: `screenings:read`, `worker:read`

params.name: worker_profile_id
params.description: Worker's HRIS profile public ID client requests to fetch KYC details for. This parameter is mutually exclusive with profile_id

params.name: contract_id
params.description: Worker's contract ID. This parameter is required in case there are multiple profiles associated to the target worker. For example, when worker used to work for the former company he had another profile ID. So we need contract ID to properly resolve profile's KYC details.  In case this parameter is not provided and multiple associated profiles are found, exception with 409 status code will be thrown

params.name: profile_id
params.description: Worker's profile public ID client requests to fetch KYC details for, This parameter is mutually exclusive with worker_profile_id

items.docs:
This endpoint allows organizations managing workers on Deel to retrieve detailed KYC verification data using the worker’s profile ID. It provides comprehensive information including verification status, document type, submission, approval, rejection, and expiry dates, supporting compliance monitoring and onboarding workflows
 **Token scopes**: `screenings:read`
